task: Task 1.1 — Core Contribution of the Paper

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Help me understand what Theorem 3.1 in Livni et al. 2012 is actually saying — break down the equivalence between RSVM2(σ) and (ℓ₂)∞SVM(σ) in plain English
full_llm_interaction_log.response_summary: The AI explained that Theorem 3.1 proves that minimizing the expected multiclass hinge loss under stochastic perturbations (RSVM2) is mathematically equivalent to minimizing the hinge loss with an explicit (ℓ₂)∞ regularizer. In other words, if you assume an adversary can randomly perturb each data point within an expected ℓ₂ ball of radius σ, then training against that adversary automatically produces the same solution as adding ℓ₂,∞ norm regularization on the weight matrix W.
full_llm_interaction_log.what_it_helped_with: Clarified that the stochastic adversary framework naturally produces ℓ₂,∞ regularization without manually adding it
full_llm_interaction_log.verification_method: Traced through the proof steps in Section 3 myself, following Eq. 6 through 13

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Help me structure my step-by-step method description in markdown — I want to describe the paper's method from input to output in clear steps with equation references
full_llm_interaction_log.response_summary: The AI provided a skeleton structure with numbered steps and placeholder markdown headings: (1) Problem setup — multiclass classification with W ∈ R^{k×d}, (2) Define stochastic adversary, (3) Formulate RSVM2 objective, (4) Apply Theorem 3.1 to rewrite as (ℓ₂)∞SVM, (5) Optimize via subgradient or CVX.
full_llm_interaction_log.what_it_helped_with: Gave me a good skeleton structure to organize my thoughts, I then filled in the actual content from my reading of the paper
full_llm_interaction_log.verification_method: Rewrote every step in my own words after understanding the flow

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: What is the geometric meaning of σ in RSVM2? The paper says it's related to perturbation radius but I want to make sure I explain it correctly
full_llm_interaction_log.response_summary: The AI confirmed that σ controls the expected ℓ₂ deviation of the stochastic perturbation from the original point. Geometrically, it defines the size of the uncertainty cloud around each data point. The paper's Section 5 proposes a heuristic where σ is set based on average nearest-neighbor distances, linking it to the local density of the data.
full_llm_interaction_log.what_it_helped_with: Confirmed that σ is the expected ℓ₂ deviation from the original point, and in Section 5 the heuristic relates it to nearest-neighbor distances
